source_pr.title: plan: fix a bug about doing aggregation plan.
source_pr.body:
When we do stream agg plan, the child of it may be a nil plan with max cost. At that time, enforceProperty function will raise a panic. Instead, we should handle this case in enforceProperty to make it more sound.
@shenli @coocood @zimulala @XuHuaiyu @tiancaiamao PTAL

symptoms: sql: "select count(*) from t group by e order by d limit 1", (plan/plan_test.go)
failure_signature: sql: "select count(*) from t group by e order by d limit 1", (plan/plan_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: sql: "select count(*) from t group by e order by d limit 1", (plan/plan_test.go)
